subject: Chemistry
solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>BeH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is indeed considered to be less stable compared to other hydrides among the given due to its significant covalent character and electron deficiency. Beryllium, being a small and highly charged cation, attracts the electrons in the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Be</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math> bonds more strongly, resulting in a greater degree of electron sharing between beryllium and hydrogen. This covalent character is in contrast to other alkaline earth metal hydrides, which tend to have more ionic character.

solution:
$\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ acts as a reducing agent only in presence of strong oxidising agents (i.e., $\left.\mathrm{MnO}_4^{-}\right)$in acidic as well as alkaline medium.
$$
\begin{aligned}
&2 \mathrm{KMnO}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+5 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow \\
&\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{MnSO}_4+8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+5 \mathrm{O}_2
\end{aligned}
$$

solution:
The isotopes of hydrogen are protium, deuterium, and tritium, which have different physical properties. For example, deuterium has a higher boiling point and a lower vapor pressure than protium, while tritium is radioactive and has a much shorter half-life than the other two isotopes.

The mass difference between the isotopes of hydrogen is indeed significant, with protium having a mass of 1 atomic mass unit (amu), deuterium having a mass of 2 amu, and tritium having a mass of 3 amu.

So Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The balanced chemical equation for the reaction of iron with steam is:

$3Fe + 4H_2O \rightarrow Fe_3O_4 + 4H_2$

Molar mass of $H_2O = 2 \times 1 + 16 = 18 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Number of moles of steam ($H_2O$) given = $\dfrac{18}{18} = 1 \text{ mol}$

From the balanced equation, $4 \text{ moles}$ of $H_2O$ react with $3 \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Therefore, $1 \text{ mole}$ of $H_2O$ will react with $\dfrac{3}{4} \text{ moles}$ of $Fe$.

Mass of iron reacted = Moles of $Fe \times$ Molar mass of $Fe$

Mass of iron = $\dfrac{3}{4} \times 56 = 3 \times 14 = 42 \text{ g}$

Answer: $42$ g

solution:
Loss of an electron from a hydrogen atom results in a nucleus of very small size as compared to other atoms or ions. Due to its small size it cannot exist free. Loss of the electron from hydrogen atom results in nucleus size (H+) of 1.5×10-3.

A proton is a positively charged subatomic particle that is present in the nucleus of an atom, and it can exist in various forms, including as a part of an atomic nucleus, as a free particle, or as a constituent of a larger particle such as a molecule. H+ always exists in combined form.

Both A and R are correct but there is no relation between two statements.

Here, the H2O2 act as the reducing agent, as the Cl has +1 oxidation state of in NaOCl and after reaction with the H2O2, in the presence of the acid, it reduces the Cl in NaOCl to Cl- having -1 oxidation state.

So, as Cl oxidation state decreases from the +1 to -1 oxidation state, hence the H2O2 is acting as the reducing agent.

Carbon monoxide does not react with water to form an acid.

The carbon monoxide molecule is one that won't react with water at all. As the acidic oxide has a higher oxidation state, it reacts with water to produce acid, and reacts with base to produce salt.

solution: The difference between the zeolite and ion exchange process is that the zeolite process uses the mineral zeolite as the exchanging resin for cations in the hard water whereas the ion exchange process include several different resins for the ion exchange. Synthetic resins method is more efficient as it can exchange both cations and anions.

solution:
It acts as an oxidising as well as reducing agent in both acidic and alkaline media. Simple reactions are described below.

(i) Oxidising action in acidic medium

2Fe2+aq+2H+aq+H2O2aq→2Fe3+aq+2H2Ol

(ii) Reducing action in acidic medium

2MnO4−+6H++5H2O2→2Mn2++8H2O+5O2

(iii) Oxidising action in basic medium

2Fe2++H2O2→2Fe3++2OH-

(iv) Reducing action in basic medium

I2+H2O2+2OH−→2I−+2H2O+O2

Density of H2O2=1.45 g/cc
